name: Dis-Chem Pharmacies Limited
sector: Retail — Pharmacy / Health & Beauty + Pharmaceutical Wholesale & Distribution

document_hosting.host: thevault.exchange (linked from dischemgroup.com financial-results page via obfuscated 'the-vault' redirect links)
document_hosting.note: The dischemgroup.com/investors/financial-results page lists docs by year tab (2026, 2025, ...) and by ANNUAL vs INTERIM. Anchor href is a base64-encoded /wp-json/the-vault/... redirect to thevault.exchange. To download: click the anchor (it navigates the tab to the thevault.exchange PDF), then fetch same-origin OR use the PDF viewer's download button. The viewer download bypasses Chrome's per-site 'multiple downloads' gate; scripted blob-downloads of a 2nd file from thevault.exchange are gated until the user clicks Allow.

operating_segments: Retail: Dis-Chem pharmacy stores (health, beauty, personal care, dispensary) + Baby City (baby) + The Local Choice (TLC) franchise model
Wholesale: Dis-Chem Distribution / CJ Distribution — supplies own stores + external independent pharmacies and TLC franchisees
Emerging: 'X, bigly labs' (data/analytics/AI + ecosystem) and Dis-Chem Life (financial services, launched Nov 2025)

key_people.chairman: J.S. Mthimunye (independent non-executive, per FY2026 AFS)
key_people.ceo: Rui Manuel Morais (CEO since July 2023; previously CFO 2012-2023)
key_people.cfo: Julia Denise Pope (CA(SA), British; CFO since 2023)
key_people.founders: Ivan & Lynette Saltzman (IL & LF Saltzman). IL Saltzman to retire 30 June 2026; SE Saltzman appointed non-exec 27 Feb 2026
key_people.auditors: Forvis Mazars (signing partner Danielle Keeve) — note: relatively recent auditor

notes: JSE: DCP. SA's #2 retail pharmacy (closest competitor to Clicks/CLS). Founder-led heritage now under professional management (Morais). FY2026 reported earnings depressed by heavy upfront 'ecosystem' investment (R445m incl X, bigly labs R330m + Dis-Chem Life) and the absence of a prior-year once-off property gain — core retail PBT (ex those) +27.1%. Subsidiaries in Namibia (Swakopmund, Walvis Bay, Wernhill) and Botswana (Airport, Game City). Better Rewards loyalty programme relaunched FY2026.
download_note: Originals downloaded 2026-06-07 via browser (Claude in Chrome) into the workspace, each with a pdftotext -layout sidecar: FY2026 AFS (audited, 87pp) + FY2026 Analyst Presentation (40pp). Binary channel = browser (web_fetch is text-only; bash curl is proxy-blocked). H1 FY2026 interim docs not yet captured (see manifest coverage_gaps).

next_expected_results.notes: Six months to ~end August 2026; published ~late October.

coverage_gaps: FY2026 SENS short-form results announcement (summary financial-summary table) not captured — the full audited AFS IS captured; grab the lighter SENS short-form from sharedata/Moneyweb if needed.
H1 FY2026 interim results + interim presentation not captured (IR page year tab 2026, INTERIM section, thevault.exchange group 6262).
FY2026 Integrated Annual Report (IAR) and remuneration report not yet published/captured.
Prior-year (FY2025) AFS + presentation not captured for multi-year trend building.
